title: 80% Exhaust Blockage Detected
shortDescription: The dryer has detected approximately 80% blockage in the exhaust vent system.
fullDescription: The d80 error code indicates the dryer's airflow sensor has detected approximately 80% blockage in the exhaust vent system. This is a serious restriction that significantly reduces drying efficiency, increases energy consumption, and poses a fire hazard. The dryer may still operate but drying times will be greatly extended. This is the first level of LG's flow sense vent blockage warning system.

causes.cause: Lint buildup in the exhaust vent duct
Crushed or kinked exhaust vent hose behind the dryer
Bird nest or debris blocking the exterior vent hood
Excessively long vent run with too many elbows
Lint screen not cleaned regularly

symptoms: d80 displayed on the control panel
Clothes take much longer than normal to dry
Dryer exterior feels very hot to the touch
Excessive humidity in the laundry room
Lint accumulating around the dryer area

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint screen
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lint screen from the dryer and clean all lint off both sides. Wash the screen with warm soapy water every few months to remove dryer sheet residue that blocks airflow through the mesh.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the vent hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dryer away from the wall. Disconnect the exhaust vent hose from the back of the dryer and from the wall duct. Inspect for lint buildup, kinks, or crushing. Use a rigid or semi-rigid metal vent hose, never a plastic or vinyl one.

fixSteps.title: Clean the full vent duct
fixSteps.instruction: Using a dryer vent cleaning kit (long flexible brush), clean the entire vent duct from the dryer connection to the exterior vent hood. Also clean from the exterior vent hood inward. Remove any accumulated lint.

fixSteps.title: Check the exterior vent hood
fixSteps.instruction: Go outside and locate the dryer vent hood on the exterior wall. Remove any bird nests, debris, or lint. Ensure the flap opens freely when air flows through. Replace the hood if the flap is broken or stuck.

fixSteps.title: Verify vent duct length and configuration
fixSteps.instruction: LG recommends a maximum vent duct length of 35 feet with a 4-inch diameter rigid duct. Each 90-degree elbow reduces the effective length by 5 feet. If your run exceeds this, consider rerouting or installing a booster fan.

whenToCallPro: If the vent run is very long, goes through walls, or you cannot access the full length for cleaning. Professional dryer vent cleaning services typically cost $100-200.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer d80 vent blockage clean exhaust

faq.q: Is d80 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, a blocked dryer vent is a fire hazard. Lint is highly flammable and restricted airflow causes overheating. Clean the vent as soon as possible.

faq.q: How often should I clean my dryer vent?
faq.a: LG recommends cleaning the dryer vent at least once a year. If you do frequent laundry, every 6 months is better. Clean the lint screen before every load.

faq.q: Can I use a vinyl vent hose?
faq.a: No. Vinyl and thin foil hoses are not recommended and may be against building code. Use a rigid or semi-rigid metal vent hose for safety and best airflow.

title: 90% Exhaust Blockage Detected
shortDescription: The dryer has detected approximately 90% blockage in the exhaust vent system.
fullDescription: The d90 error code indicates approximately 90% blockage in the dryer's exhaust vent system. This is a critical restriction level that can cause the dryer to overheat, trip thermal safety devices, and create a significant fire risk. The dryer may shut down mid-cycle to protect itself. Immediate vent cleaning is required.

causes.cause: Severe lint accumulation in the exhaust vent duct
Completely crushed vent hose behind the dryer
Exterior vent hood completely blocked or sealed
Vent duct disconnected inside wall creating lint cavity

symptoms: d90 displayed on the control panel
Dryer shuts down mid-cycle due to overheating
Clothes extremely hot but still damp
Strong heat radiating from the dryer and surrounding area
Burning smell from the dryer area

fixSteps.title: Stop using the dryer immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Do not run the dryer again until the vent is cleared. A 90% blockage is a fire hazard. Unplug the dryer or turn off the gas valve if it is a gas model.

fixSteps.title: Disconnect and inspect the vent hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dryer away from the wall. Disconnect the vent hose from both the dryer and the wall duct connection. Check the hose for complete blockage and replace if crushed or damaged.

fixSteps.title: Clean the entire vent system
fixSteps.instruction: Using a dryer vent brush kit, thoroughly clean the duct from the dryer connection to the exterior. Work from both ends. Use a vacuum to capture loose lint. Consider using a leaf blower from the dryer end to push lint out the exterior.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the dryer interior
fixSteps.instruction: With the dryer unplugged, remove the lint screen housing (typically two screws). Use a vacuum with a long narrow attachment to clean lint from inside the lint screen cavity and around the blower housing.

whenToCallPro: d90 indicates a near-complete blockage. If you cannot clear the vent yourself or if the duct runs through walls or ceilings, hire a professional vent cleaning service.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer d90 vent blockage clean exhaust duct

faq.q: Can I just ignore d90 and keep drying?
faq.a: Absolutely not. A 90% vent blockage is a serious fire hazard. Dryer fires cause approximately 2,900 home fires per year in the US. Stop using the dryer until the vent is cleaned.

faq.q: Why did my dryer shut off mid-cycle with d90?
faq.a: The dryer has thermal safety devices that shut it down when temperatures exceed safe limits. The blocked vent prevents hot moist air from escaping, causing dangerous overheating.

faq.q: Can a new dryer still get d90?
faq.a: Yes, d90 is about the vent system, not the dryer itself. Even a brand new dryer will show d90 if the vent duct is blocked. Always check the vent before installing a new dryer.

title: 95% Exhaust Blockage - Critical
shortDescription: The dryer has detected a near-complete blockage of the exhaust vent system.
fullDescription: The d95 error code indicates the exhaust vent system is essentially completely blocked, with approximately 95% or greater restriction. This is the most critical level of LG's flow sense vent blockage detection. The dryer will likely refuse to operate or will shut down almost immediately after starting. This is a dangerous condition requiring immediate attention to prevent a dryer fire.

causes.cause: Complete lint blockage in the exhaust vent duct
Vent duct disconnected or separated at a joint
Exterior vent hood completely sealed shut by paint, ice, or debris
Vent hose not connected to the dryer or wall at all

symptoms: d95 displayed on the control panel
Dryer refuses to start or shuts down within minutes
Extreme heat buildup in the laundry area
Visible lint accumulation around the dryer or wall vent
Condensation or moisture inside the dryer

fixSteps.title: Do not operate the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: A 95% blockage is a critical fire hazard. Do not attempt to run the dryer until the vent is completely cleared. Unplug the dryer or turn off the gas supply immediately.

fixSteps.title: Check vent hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the vent hose is connected to both the dryer exhaust port and the wall duct. A disconnected hose will read as complete blockage to the flow sensor. Reconnect and secure with clamps.

fixSteps.title: Clean or replace the entire vent run
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the vent hose. Clean the entire duct system from dryer to exterior. If the duct is old, damaged, or inaccessible, consider having the entire run replaced with new rigid 4-inch metal duct.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for duct separation
fixSteps.instruction: If the vent runs through walls or ceiling, check for separated joints or collapsed sections. You may need to access attic or crawlspace areas. A disconnected duct inside a wall is a major fire hazard as lint accumulates in the wall cavity.

partsNeeded.part: Rigid Metal Vent Duct (4 inch, per 8 ft section)

whenToCallPro: d95 strongly suggests a professional vent cleaning or replacement is needed, especially if the duct runs through walls, attic, or is longer than 15 feet.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer d95 critical vent blockage clean replace

faq.q: Is d95 a fire hazard?
faq.a: Yes, d95 represents an extremely dangerous condition. A nearly complete vent blockage is one of the top causes of residential dryer fires. Do not use the dryer until resolved.

faq.q: Can I just remove the vent and vent into the room?
faq.a: No. Venting a dryer indoors introduces moisture, lint, and (for gas dryers) carbon monoxide into your home. Always vent to the exterior through a proper duct.

faq.q: Should I have my vents professionally inspected?
faq.a: Yes, especially if you get d90 or d95 errors. A professional can use cameras to inspect the full duct run, identify separations inside walls, and ensure proper installation.

title: Exhaust Thermistor Error
shortDescription: The exhaust temperature sensor (thermistor) has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The tE1 error code indicates the exhaust thermistor, which monitors the temperature of air leaving the dryer drum, has failed or is providing abnormal readings. This sensor is critical for preventing overheating and controlling drying cycles. The dryer will not operate without a functioning exhaust thermistor.

causes.cause: Defective exhaust thermistor (open or shorted)
Loose or corroded thermistor wiring connection
Damaged wiring harness between thermistor and control board
Main control board thermistor input failure

symptoms: tE1 displayed on the control panel
Dryer will not start
Dryer shuts down shortly after starting
Incorrect temperature regulation during drying

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes and plug it back in. A transient sensor error may clear with a reset. Try starting a timed dry cycle.

fixSteps.title: Locate the exhaust thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. The exhaust thermistor is typically located in the exhaust duct area inside the dryer, near where hot air exits the drum. Remove the rear panel or top panel to access it (varies by model).

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor wiring. Using a multimeter, measure the resistance. At room temperature (77F/25C), it should read approximately 10,000 ohms (10k). If it reads 0 or infinity, it has failed and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the faulty thermistor from its mounting bracket (usually a single screw or clip). Install the new thermistor in the same position, reconnect the wiring harness, and reassemble the panel.

whenToCallPro: If you are uncomfortable accessing the internal components of the dryer. The repair itself is straightforward once accessible.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer tE1 exhaust thermistor replacement

faq.q: What is the difference between tE1, tE2, and tE3?
faq.a: tE1 is the exhaust thermistor, tE2 is the inlet thermistor, and tE3 is the drum thermistor. Each monitors temperature at a different point in the airflow path.

faq.q: Can a blocked vent cause tE1?
faq.a: Indirectly. A blocked vent can cause extreme heat that damages the thermistor over time. Always check and clean the vent when replacing a thermistor.

faq.q: How much does a thermistor cost?
faq.a: LG dryer thermistors are very inexpensive, typically $5-15 for the part. The main professional repair cost is labor to access and replace it.

title: Inlet Thermistor Error
shortDescription: The inlet air temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The tE2 error code indicates the inlet thermistor, which monitors the temperature of air entering the dryer drum, has failed. This sensor helps the dryer regulate heating by measuring the temperature of incoming air. Without it, the dryer cannot properly control the heater.

causes.cause: Defective inlet thermistor
Loose wiring connection at the thermistor
Wiring harness damage from heat exposure
Control board input circuit failure

symptoms: tE2 displayed on the control panel
Dryer will not start or heater does not activate
Clothes not drying due to no heat
Error appears at the beginning of the cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes and plug it back in. Try running a Timed Dry cycle to see if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Access the inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. The inlet thermistor is located near the heater housing or blower inlet area. Remove the appropriate panel (usually rear) to access it.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor wiring. Measure resistance with a multimeter. It should read approximately 10,000 ohms at room temperature. Replace if the reading is 0, infinite, or significantly off.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting hardware and swap the faulty thermistor for the new one. Reconnect the wiring harness and reassemble. Test the dryer.

whenToCallPro: If accessing the thermistor requires significant disassembly or if you suspect the control board is at fault.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer tE2 inlet thermistor error fix

faq.q: Can I use the dryer without the inlet thermistor?
faq.a: No, the dryer will not operate properly without a functioning inlet thermistor. It is needed for safe temperature regulation.

faq.q: Are tE1 and tE2 thermistors the same part?
faq.a: They may use the same part number on some models, but they are located in different positions. Always verify the correct location before replacement.

faq.q: Why do thermistors fail?
faq.a: Thermistors can fail due to age, repeated exposure to extreme heat (often from blocked vents), electrical surges, or manufacturing defects.

title: Drum Thermistor Error
shortDescription: The drum temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The tE3 error code indicates the drum thermistor has failed. This sensor measures the temperature inside the drum itself and provides feedback to the control board for precise cycle management. It is particularly important for sensor dry cycles that automatically determine when clothes are dry.

causes.cause: Defective drum thermistor
Loose or disconnected thermistor wiring
Heat damage to the wiring near the heater
Control board sensor input failure

symptoms: tE3 displayed on the control panel
Sensor dry cycles do not work properly
Dryer may not start
Clothes over-dried or under-dried on auto cycles

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes. Plug it back in and try a Timed Dry cycle first, then test a Sensor Dry cycle.

fixSteps.title: Locate the drum thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. The drum thermistor is typically mounted on the blower housing or inside the drum area. Access may require removing the rear panel and/or drum depending on the model.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the wiring and measure resistance with a multimeter (expect ~10,000 ohms at room temperature). If faulty, remove the mounting hardware and install the new thermistor.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring integrity
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the entire wiring path from the thermistor to the control board for heat damage, pinched wires, or disconnected connectors. Repair or replace any damaged sections.

whenToCallPro: If accessing the drum thermistor requires drum removal, which can be labor-intensive on some models.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer tE3 drum thermistor error replacement

faq.q: Will timed dry cycles still work with tE3?
faq.a: Some models may allow timed dry to operate even with tE3, but the dryer may not regulate temperature correctly. Repair is still recommended.

faq.q: Can a blocked vent damage the drum thermistor?
faq.a: Yes, excessive heat from a blocked vent can degrade or destroy thermistors over time. Always maintain proper ventilation.

faq.q: How do I know which thermistor is the drum one?
faq.a: Consult your model's tech sheet (usually found inside the control panel or behind the front lower panel). It shows the location and wiring diagram for each thermistor.

title: Moisture Sensor Error
shortDescription: The dryer's moisture sensor bars are not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: The E13 error code indicates the moisture sensor system has failed. LG dryers use two metal sensor bars inside the drum that detect the moisture level of clothes tumbling against them. When these sensors are coated with dryer sheet residue, corroded, or electrically disconnected, the dryer cannot determine when clothes are dry, affecting auto-dry cycle accuracy.

causes.cause: Dryer sheet residue buildup on moisture sensor bars
Faulty moisture sensor connection or wiring
Corroded or damaged moisture sensor bars
Control board moisture detection circuit failure

symptoms: E13 displayed on the control panel
Auto dry cycles end too early (clothes still damp)
Auto dry cycles run far too long
Sensor dry feature does not function

fixSteps.title: Clean the moisture sensor bars
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door and locate the two metal sensor bars inside the drum (usually near the lint screen opening). Clean them with a soft cloth dampened with rubbing alcohol. Scrub gently to remove the invisible film of dryer sheet residue.

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint screen housing
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lint screen and use a vacuum to clean inside the lint screen housing. Dryer sheet residue can also accumulate here and affect sensor performance.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the front lower panel to access the moisture sensor wire connections. Ensure the wires are securely connected and not damaged. The sensor bars connect via two wires to the control board.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor wires from the control board. Place a damp cloth across both sensor bars and measure resistance with a multimeter. The reading should change from infinite (dry) to a measurable resistance (wet). If no change occurs, the sensors or wiring need replacement.

whenToCallPro: Usually not necessary. Cleaning the sensor bars with rubbing alcohol resolves this issue in most cases.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer E13 moisture sensor clean fix

faq.q: Do dryer sheets really cause sensor problems?
faq.a: Yes, fabric softener dryer sheets leave an invisible waxy residue on the moisture sensor bars over time. This coating prevents the sensors from detecting moisture accurately.

faq.q: Should I stop using dryer sheets?
faq.a: Using fewer dryer sheets and cleaning the sensor bars monthly with rubbing alcohol will help. Alternatively, use liquid fabric softener in the washer instead of dryer sheets.

faq.q: Can I use timed dry instead of auto dry?
faq.a: Yes, timed dry does not rely on the moisture sensors and can be used as a workaround while you address the sensor issue.

title: Power Supply Error
shortDescription: The dryer is not receiving the correct voltage from the electrical supply.
fullDescription: The PS error code indicates the dryer is detecting a problem with its power supply. Electric dryers require a 240V circuit with two hot legs. If one leg loses power (due to a tripped breaker, loose connection, or wiring issue), the dryer may receive only 120V. The drum will tumble but the heater will not activate. Gas dryers showing PS may have a different electrical supply issue.

causes.cause: One leg of the 240V circuit breaker has tripped
Loose connection in the electrical outlet or cord
Faulty 240V electrical outlet
Power cord damage or loose terminal block connection

symptoms: PS displayed on the control panel
Drum tumbles but no heat is produced
Dryer controls work but drying is ineffective
Display is dim or flickering

fixSteps.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Go to your electrical panel and find the double-pole breaker for the dryer (usually 30A). Even if it appears to be on, flip it fully OFF and then back ON. Sometimes one pole trips while the other stays on.

fixSteps.title: Test the outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter set to AC voltage, test the dryer outlet. Between the two hot slots you should read 220-240V. Between each hot slot and neutral, you should read 110-120V. If one side is dead, the breaker or wiring is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Check the power cord connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Remove the terminal block access panel on the back of the dryer. Verify that all three or four power cord wires are securely fastened to the terminal block. Tighten any loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the power cord
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the entire power cord for damage, burns, or melting, especially near the plug and where it connects to the dryer. Replace the cord if any damage is found.

whenToCallPro: If the circuit breaker keeps tripping or if you find low voltage at the outlet, call a licensed electrician to inspect the wiring.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer PS power supply error 240V fix

faq.q: Why does my dryer tumble but not heat with PS?
faq.a: The drum motor runs on 120V while the heater requires 240V. If one leg of the 240V supply is lost, the motor works but the heater cannot activate.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause PS?
faq.a: A power surge can trip the circuit breaker, which then shows as PS. Reset the breaker at the electrical panel.

faq.q: Is PS common on gas dryers?
faq.a: Less common, as gas dryers only need 120V for the motor and controls. PS on a gas dryer typically indicates a loose plug or outlet issue.

title: No Power to Heater
shortDescription: The dryer detects insufficient voltage to operate the heating element.
fullDescription: The nP error code specifically indicates the dryer is not receiving adequate power to run the heating element. This is similar to PS but more specifically targets the heater circuit. For electric dryers, this means the 240V supply is compromised. For gas dryers, it may indicate an issue with the gas ignition circuit.

causes.cause: One leg of the 240V circuit has failed (electric dryers)
Tripped circuit breaker
Faulty heating element relay on the control board
Blown thermal fuse cutting power to the heater

symptoms: nP displayed on the control panel
Dryer runs but produces no heat
Extended drying times with cold air
Error may appear after a power outage

fixSteps.title: Reset the circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the dryer circuit breaker fully OFF and then ON at the electrical panel. For double-pole breakers, push both poles firmly to ensure they are fully engaged.

fixSteps.title: Verify outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Test the dryer outlet with a multimeter. Electric dryers need 220-240V between the two hot terminals. If the voltage is low or absent on one leg, the issue is in the home wiring.

fixSteps.title: Check the thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heater housing and locate the thermal fuse (a small device with two wires). Test for continuity with a multimeter. If open (no continuity), the fuse has blown and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the heater wires and measure resistance across the heating element terminals. It should read 10-30 ohms for electric dryers. An open reading means the element has burned out.

whenToCallPro: If the home electrical supply is the issue, call an electrician. If the control board relay is suspected, a dryer technician is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer nP no power heater error fix

faq.q: Is nP the same as PS?
faq.a: They are similar but nP specifically focuses on the heater circuit, while PS is a more general power supply error. Both can be caused by a tripped breaker on a 240V circuit.

faq.q: Why did my thermal fuse blow?
faq.a: Thermal fuses blow when the dryer overheats, almost always due to a restricted exhaust vent. After replacing the fuse, always clean the vent to prevent it from blowing again.

faq.q: Can I bypass the thermal fuse?
faq.a: Never bypass a thermal fuse. It is a critical safety device that prevents dryer fires. If it has blown, replace it and fix the underlying cause (usually a blocked vent).

title: Child Lock Activated
shortDescription: The child lock feature is engaged, preventing button presses.
fullDescription: CL is not an error code but a status indicator showing the child lock feature is active on the dryer. When CL is displayed, all control panel buttons are disabled except the Power button. This is a safety feature to prevent children from accidentally starting the dryer or changing settings.

causes.cause: Child lock intentionally activated
Child lock accidentally activated while cleaning the panel
Sticky or malfunctioning control panel buttons
Control board glitch

symptoms: CL displayed on the control panel
No buttons respond except Power
Cannot start or adjust dryer settings
Beeping when buttons are pressed

fixSteps.title: Deactivate child lock
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the Child Lock button (or the designated button combination) for 3-5 seconds until CL disappears and the dryer beeps. Check the control panel for a small lock icon indicating the correct buttons.

fixSteps.title: Try common button combinations
fixSteps.instruction: If no dedicated Child Lock button exists, try holding Dry Level + Time for 3 seconds, or Temp + Time for 3 seconds. The combination varies by model.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: If the child lock will not deactivate, turn the dryer off with the Power button. Unplug it for 1 minute, then plug it back in and try again.

fixSteps.title: Check the user manual
fixSteps.instruction: Consult your specific model's user manual for the exact child lock button combination. You can download the manual from LG's support website using your model number.

whenToCallPro: Only if the child lock will not deactivate after power cycling, which may indicate a control panel issue.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer CL child lock turn off deactivate

faq.q: Is CL an error?
faq.a: No, CL stands for Child Lock and is a deliberate safety feature. Press and hold the designated buttons for 3 seconds to deactivate it.

faq.q: Why does CL keep turning on?
faq.a: Check if someone is accidentally pressing the child lock buttons. If it activates on its own, the control panel membrane may have a fault.

faq.q: Can the dryer still run with CL on?
faq.a: No, the child lock prevents starting the dryer and changing any settings. Deactivate it first.

title: Humidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The humidity sensor is malfunctioning or providing erratic readings.
fullDescription: The HS error code indicates the dryer's humidity sensor system is not working correctly. This sensor monitors the moisture content of the air inside the drum to determine when clothes are dry. When it fails, auto-dry cycles cannot function properly, either ending too early or running too long.

causes.cause: Dirty or coated humidity sensor
Faulty humidity sensor module
Wiring connection issue to the sensor
Control board sensor circuit failure

symptoms: HS displayed on the control panel
Auto dry cycles do not work correctly
Dryer runs too long or not long enough on auto settings
Sensor dry light flashes or does not illuminate

fixSteps.title: Clean the moisture sensor bars
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door. Locate the two metal sensor bars inside the drum near the lint trap opening. Clean them thoroughly with rubbing alcohol and a soft cloth to remove invisible residue from dryer sheets.

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes. Plug it back in and test with a sensor dry cycle using a damp towel.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the sensor connections inside the dryer (usually behind the front lower panel). Verify the sensor wires are securely connected and undamaged.

fixSteps.title: Test or replace the humidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, the sensor module may need replacement. On some models, this is the same as the moisture sensor bar assembly. Replace the sensor and test.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the sensors does not fix the issue and the wiring and connections appear intact.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer HS humidity sensor error clean fix

faq.q: Can I use timed dry instead?
faq.a: Yes, timed dry does not use the humidity sensor and can be used as a temporary workaround while you address the HS error.

faq.q: How often should I clean the sensor bars?
faq.a: Clean the sensor bars with rubbing alcohol once a month, or more frequently if you use dryer sheets regularly.

faq.q: Does the humidity sensor affect timed dry?
faq.a: No, timed dry cycles run for a fixed time regardless of the humidity sensor. Only sensor dry and auto dry cycles are affected.

title: Power Failure
shortDescription: The dryer experienced a power interruption during a cycle.
fullDescription: The PF error code indicates a power failure or fluctuation occurred while the dryer was running a cycle. This is not a machine malfunction but an indication of an external power interruption. The dryer remembers its cycle position and can resume when power is restored.

causes.cause: Power outage or brownout during operation
Loose electrical plug connection
Tripped circuit breaker
Faulty electrical outlet or home wiring

symptoms: PF displayed after power is restored
Dryer stopped mid-cycle
Display resets or flashes
Cycle does not resume automatically

fixSteps.title: Press Start to resume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Start/Pause button to resume the drying cycle from where it was interrupted.

fixSteps.title: Check power connections
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the power cord is firmly plugged into the wall outlet. Wiggle the plug to check for a loose connection. A loose plug can cause intermittent power loss.

fixSteps.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Go to the electrical panel and verify the dryer circuit breaker is fully in the ON position. If it tripped, reset it by pushing it fully OFF and then back ON.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for electrical issues
fixSteps.instruction: If PF occurs repeatedly with no known power outages, the outlet or home wiring may be faulty. Have a licensed electrician inspect the circuit.

whenToCallPro: Only if PF errors occur frequently without known power outages, indicating a possible wiring issue requiring an electrician.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer PF power failure error fix

faq.q: Do I need to restart the whole cycle?
faq.a: No, pressing Start will resume from where the cycle was interrupted.

faq.q: Can PF damage my dryer?
faq.a: Occasional power failures will not damage the dryer. Frequent surges may affect the control board over time.

faq.q: Should I use a surge protector?
faq.a: Standard surge protectors are not rated for 240V dryer circuits. Use a whole-house surge protector installed at your electrical panel instead.

title: Frequency Error / Speed Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The dryer motor speed sensor has detected an abnormal drum rotation frequency.
fullDescription: The F1 error code indicates the dryer's motor speed sensor is detecting an abnormal rotation frequency or is unable to detect the drum's rotation at all. This could mean the drum is not spinning, spinning too fast, or the speed sensor has failed. The dryer needs to monitor drum speed for proper cycle operation and safety.

causes.cause: Worn or broken drum drive belt
Faulty motor speed sensor
Defective drive motor
Loose belt or idler pulley issue

symptoms: F1 displayed on the control panel
Drum does not spin
Motor hums but drum does not turn
Dryer stops shortly after starting

fixSteps.title: Check the drum belt
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door and try to turn the drum by hand. If it spins very freely with no resistance, the drive belt has likely broken. You can also look for belt fragments inside the dryer.

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes and plug it back in. Try starting a timed dry cycle to see if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel and front panel to access the drum. The belt wraps around the drum, through the idler pulley, and around the motor pulley. Remove the old belt, route the new belt around the drum, through the idler pulley tensioner, and around the motor pulley in a zigzag pattern.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor and speed sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the belt is intact, access the motor and check the speed sensor mounted on it. Test the sensor with a multimeter. Also test the motor windings for proper resistance.

whenToCallPro: If the motor needs replacement or if you are not comfortable with the belt routing process.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer F1 error belt motor speed sensor fix

faq.q: How do I know if the belt is broken?
faq.a: Open the dryer door and spin the drum by hand. A drum with a broken belt will spin very freely with almost no resistance, whereas a drum with an intact belt has noticeable resistance from the motor.

faq.q: How long do dryer belts last?
faq.a: Dryer belts typically last 10-15 years under normal use. Overloading the dryer can accelerate belt wear.

faq.q: Can I replace the belt myself?
faq.a: Yes, belt replacement is one of the more common DIY dryer repairs. The belt itself is inexpensive ($8-20). The routing path around the idler pulley and motor can be tricky but there are clear diagrams for each model.

title: Door Error
shortDescription: The dryer door is not properly closed or the door switch has failed.
fullDescription: The dE error code indicates the dryer cannot detect that the door is securely closed. This prevents the dryer from starting as a safety measure. The door switch is a simple mechanical switch that activates when the door is closed. The dE4 variant indicates a specific door switch circuit malfunction.

causes.cause: Door not fully closed or latched
Faulty door switch
Broken door latch or catch
Damaged wiring to the door switch

symptoms: dE displayed on the control panel
Dryer will not start
Door appears closed but dryer does not recognize it
Drum light stays on with door closed

fixSteps.title: Close the door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open and close the dryer door firmly, ensuring it latches completely. You should hear a distinct click when the door catches. Check for any clothing or lint preventing the door from fully closing.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door latch (the hook on the door) and the catch (the receiver on the dryer body) for damage or wear. If the latch is bent or the catch is cracked, replace the damaged component.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Open the door and locate the door switch (usually accessible from inside the front panel). Remove the switch and test it with a multimeter. It should show continuity when pressed and no continuity when released.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch fails the continuity test, remove the retaining hardware and disconnect the wires (note their positions). Install the new switch, reconnect the wires, and test.

whenToCallPro: Door switch replacement is a straightforward DIY repair. A professional is rarely needed unless wiring is damaged.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer dE door error switch replacement

faq.q: What is the difference between dE and dE4?
faq.a: dE is a general door error indicating the door is not detected as closed. dE4 specifically indicates the door switch circuit has a malfunction, such as a short or open circuit.

faq.q: Why does my dryer door not stay closed?
faq.a: The door catch or latch may be worn. The catch is a small plastic piece that the door hook grabs onto. Over time it can wear down and no longer hold the door securely.

faq.q: Is the door switch expensive?
faq.a: No, door switches are one of the least expensive dryer parts, typically $5-15. The repair takes about 15 minutes.

title: Network Error (Smart ThinQ)
shortDescription: The dryer's Wi-Fi module or smart connectivity feature has an error.
fullDescription: The nE error code indicates the dryer's built-in Wi-Fi module is experiencing a connectivity issue. This only affects smart features like remote monitoring through the LG ThinQ app and Smart Diagnosis. The dryer operates normally for drying functions regardless of this error.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi module initialization failure
Home network configuration change
Faulty Wi-Fi module hardware
Software glitch in the network processor

symptoms: nE displayed on the control panel
Cannot connect to LG ThinQ app
Wi-Fi indicator not lit
Smart Diagnosis does not function

fixSteps.title: Reset Wi-Fi module
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the Wi-Fi button for 3 seconds to reset the wireless module. Wait for the Wi-Fi indicator to flash.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 2 minutes and plug back in. This resets the Wi-Fi chip. Then reconnect through the LG ThinQ app.

fixSteps.title: Check Wi-Fi network
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your router is working and broadcasting on 2.4 GHz (not 5 GHz only). Ensure the dryer is within range of the router.

fixSteps.title: Re-register in ThinQ
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the dryer from the ThinQ app. Factory reset the dryer's Wi-Fi using the button combination specified in your manual. Then re-add the dryer through the app setup process.

whenToCallPro: Only if the Wi-Fi module hardware has failed. The dryer works fine for drying without Wi-Fi.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer nE network WiFi error ThinQ fix

faq.q: Can I still dry clothes with nE?
faq.a: Yes, nE only affects smart connectivity. All drying functions work normally.

faq.q: Does the dryer need Wi-Fi?
faq.a: No, Wi-Fi is optional for remote monitoring and smart features only.

faq.q: Why only 2.4 GHz?
faq.a: LG smart appliances use 2.4 GHz for its better range and wall penetration compared to 5 GHz.

title: Door Switch Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: The door switch is reporting an abnormal electrical state to the control board.
fullDescription: The dE4 error code indicates a specific electrical fault in the door switch circuit. Unlike the general dE error which can mean the door is simply not closed, dE4 points to a short circuit, open circuit, or abnormal electrical reading from the door switch. This requires testing or replacing the switch and inspecting the wiring.

causes.cause: Door switch contacts welded shut (stuck closed)
Short circuit in door switch wiring
Corroded door switch contacts
Control board door input circuit failure

symptoms: dE4 displayed on the control panel
Dryer will not start even with door firmly closed
Drum light behavior is abnormal
Error appears immediately when attempting to start

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes. Plug it back in, close the door, and try starting a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access and remove the door switch. Test with a multimeter: should show no continuity with button released and continuity when pressed. If it shows constant continuity (always closed), the contacts are welded.

fixSteps.title: Inspect switch wiring
fixSteps.instruction: With the switch removed, inspect the wiring for damage, burns, or bare spots that could cause a short. Also check the connector pins for corrosion or spreading.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Install a new door switch in the same mounting location. Reconnect the wires and test the dryer.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the switch does not resolve dE4, the control board may have an issue.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer dE4 door switch circuit error fix

faq.q: Can a welded switch be dangerous?
faq.a: A switch stuck in the closed position could allow the dryer to run with the door open, which is a safety hazard. Replace it immediately.

faq.q: Is dE4 common?
faq.a: dE4 is less common than the general dE error. It specifically indicates an electrical fault rather than a simple door closure issue.

faq.q: How much does a door switch cost?
faq.a: Door switches are very inexpensive at $5-15 and are easy to replace.

title: Cool Down Cycle Active
shortDescription: The dryer is in the cool-down tumbling phase after heating.
fullDescription: The Cd code is a normal status indicator, not an error. It appears when the dryer has completed the heating portion of the cycle and is now tumbling the clothes without heat to cool them down and prevent wrinkles. This phase typically lasts 5-15 minutes. The dryer will beep when the complete cycle finishes.

causes.cause: Normal end-of-cycle cool-down period
Anti-wrinkle tumble feature active after cycle end
Extended cool-down due to high drum temperature
Temperature sensor issue extending cool-down (rare)

symptoms: Cd displayed on the control panel
Drum continues to tumble slowly
No heat is being produced
Dryer is in the final phase of the cycle

fixSteps.title: Wait for cool-down to complete
fixSteps.instruction: Allow the cool-down phase to complete naturally. It typically lasts 5-15 minutes. The dryer will signal when the full cycle is done.

fixSteps.title: Open the door to end early
fixSteps.instruction: If you need to end the cool-down early, simply open the dryer door. Remove the clothes promptly to prevent wrinkles.

fixSteps.title: Disable wrinkle prevention (optional)
fixSteps.instruction: If you do not want the extended tumbling after the cycle, check your settings for a Wrinkle Care or anti-wrinkle option and disable it before starting the next cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check vent if cool-down is excessive
fixSteps.instruction: If cool-down lasts more than 20 minutes, the dryer may be retaining excessive heat due to restricted venting. Check and clean the exhaust vent system.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Cd is normal dryer operation.
videoSearchQuery: LG dryer Cd cool down code meaning

faq.q: Is Cd an error code?
faq.a: No, Cd stands for Cool Down and is a normal part of the drying cycle. The dryer tumbles without heat to cool clothes and prevent wrinkles.

faq.q: Can I open the door during Cd?
faq.a: Yes, you can open the door at any time during cool-down to remove clothes.

faq.q: Why do my clothes still feel warm during Cd?
faq.a: The drum and clothes retain heat from the drying cycle. The cool-down phase gradually reduces this temperature. Clothes may feel warm but should not be hot.
